what_it_does: Provides territory_for(address) to compose a structured, brain-agnostic context dict for a contracts.address (identity, context items, chats, relations, notes, and leg-presence flags), plus territory_prose to render it into an operator-facing [Operator context] block, territory_label to produce a human-readable label, and territory_write/territory_directions_at for persisting and reading gallery:direction/route-back marks at an address.
observations: The module opens with a large design docstring describing laws: brain-agnostic, reuse-don't-parallel, GPU-free, degrade-contract, and lens-independence.
territory_for resolves identity per scheme using runtime.cognition.resolve_address for resolvable schemes and suite.address_help for ui://, with each leg in its own try/except.
A non-address (no '://') raises ValueError; a non-resolvable scheme or missing record degrades to a note instead of crashing.
ui://instrument/surface addresses are special-cased to load the active projection view meta from bindings/<id>.py, overriding the thin ui identity.
A corpus-record leg uses suite.read_corpus_record and suite.find_corpus as a fallback to promote real comprehended content to identity when resolve_address yields nothing.
Relations are fetched via runtime.cc_board.relations and truncated to max_relations.
vi-vision:// addresses get a capped global library palette leg.
decision:// addresses trigger a memory leg that runs recall_for_decision in a daemon thread with a hard timeout to avoid slow embed hangs.
territory_prose deliberately omits raw addresses and machine ids, surfacing only human meaning, view meta, content, context, connection counts, library names, decision options, and memory digests.
territory_write canonicalizes decision addresses for decision_take/retract, persists marks via suite.mark, and emits a decision_decided_signal for takes.
territory_directions_at reads marks via suite.marks_for and returns an empty list on error.
standouts: The degrade-contract design: every lookup leg is independently guarded so a dead leg becomes a noted-absent leg rather than a void territory.
Operator-law enforcement: raw addresses and machine identifiers are filtered out of rendered prose and labels.
The bounded daemon-thread memory leg for decision://, with a configurable hard timeout to prevent slow embed calls from hanging the resolve.
Unification of gallery direction marks and decision take/retract through a single scheme-agnostic suite.mark path plus canonical address routing and a post-mark signal.
conventions: Brain-agnostic composition: the file resolves context but leaves focus/panel decisions to downstream consumers.
Reuses existing resolvers instead of introducing parallel ones.
Fail-loud for malformed input, degrade-clean for missing or slow optional legs.
Late imports inside functions and try blocks rather than module-top imports.
Hard-coded scheme lists, binding path conventions, and mark type tuples.
Environment-overridable timeout constant for the memory leg.
concerns: The _recall helper is nested inside territory_for, uses a closure box and a daemon thread, and is hard to unit-test or reason about for cancellation and side effects.
Binding meta loads arbitrary bindings/<id>.py files from disk; while there is a basic path-traversal guard, the security model of binding files is unclear.
The daemon-thread timeout abandons recall_for_decision but does not actually cancel it, leaving a potentially long-running orphaned thread.
Late imports and broad exception catches reduce static analyzability and may hide import-time errors.
territory_prose does string slicing without grapheme or word-boundary awareness, risking truncated output.
territory_write mutates the target address for decision types and mixes the mark record with signal outcome fields, coupling read and write semantics.
The _RESOLVABLE tuple is hardcoded and must be kept in sync with cognition.resolve_address dispatch.
notes: This is the address-to-operator-context composer: it resolves, guards, and renders meaning for a given address while keeping raw identifiers out of the operator-facing prose.
questions: What exact shapes do suite.address_help, suite.context_at, suite.chats_at, suite.read_corpus_record, suite.find_corpus, suite.mark, and suite.marks_for return?
How does runtime.cc_board.relations behave when store is None for store-independent schemes?
What thread-safety and cancellation guarantees does recall_for_decision provide when run in a daemon thread?
Are bindings/<id>.py files trusted code, and what additional validation is applied beyond slash/dot checks?
How does decision_address canonicalization interact with element_id sub-addresses that contain '#'?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches what it does: it composes territories and renders operator context, with added route-back write support.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make any system address legible and actionable to an operator-facing brain turn by resolving meaning through existing registries, isolating each lookup so a failure in one subsystem does not collapse context, and providing a symmetric write path for operator reactions and decisions.
novelty: The explicit degrade contract with per-leg notes and operator-law address suppression, plus the bounded daemon-thread memory leg for decision explanations, and the unification of gallery direction marks with decision take/retract through canonical address routing and a post-mark signal.
contribution: A guarded, reusable address-to-context renderer and a scheme-agnostic route-back write helper that keeps operator-facing output free of raw addresses while tolerating missing or slow downstream legs.
summary_for_embedding: company/runtime/territory.py composes and renders a human-legible operator context block from a contracts address, guards each resolution leg with degrade-clean notes, and provides scheme-agnostic route-back mark writing including decision take/retract.
intention_for_embedding: To give brain turns a resilient, address-driven context composer that reuses existing resolvers, never exposes raw identifiers to operators, and supports bidirectional surface interactions.

symbols.does: Extracts the scheme from a contracts.address string.
symbols.description: A thin wrapper around contracts.address.scheme that returns the scheme of an address, guarded so a failure yields None rather than crashing the caller.

symbols.does: Parses the active binding id from a ui://instrument/surface address fragment.
symbols.description: Recognizes instrument surface addresses and extracts the #binding=<id> fragment, defaulting to 'raw', so the territory can ground on the active projection view meta.

symbols.does: Loads the legibility meta dict for a projection binding from bindings/<id>.py.
symbols.description: Dynamically imports a bindings module by id and returns its BINDING['meta'] dict if present and well-formed; degrades to None on any error and never raises.

symbols.does: Composes a territory dict for an address with identity, context, relations, and notes.
symbols.description: The core composer that resolves identity per scheme, adds corpus-record, context, relations, library, and memory legs, guards each independently, and records which legs succeeded.

symbols.does: Nested daemon-thread worker that runs recall_for_decision for decision:// addresses.
symbols.description: Defined inside territory_for to bound the memory leg with a hard timeout; it captures the recall result, exception, or timeout state in a closure box.

symbols.does: Renders a territory into an operator-facing [Operator context] prose block.
symbols.description: Converts a territory dict or address into a human-readable context string that suppresses raw addresses and machine ids, surfacing meaning, view meta, content, connections, library pieces, decisions, and memory digests.

symbols.does: Collapses whitespace and truncates a string to n characters with an ellipsis.
symbols.description: A small helper used throughout rendering to keep operator-facing lines compact; it normalizes whitespace before clipping.

symbols.does: Extracts a human one-liner from an identity value.
symbols.description: Walks dict identities for legibility.name and whitelisted human fields, or takes the first non-empty line of a string identity, stripping leading markdown headings.

symbols.does: Returns a short human label for an address or territory.
symbols.description: Resolves a one-line 'what-this-is' label by reusing territory_for and _human_from_identity, degrading to a scheme-specific human noun or 'this' while never returning the raw address.

symbols.does: Persists one gallery:direction item at its sub-address.
symbols.description: Routes direction marks to suite.mark, canonicalizes decision addresses for take/retract, normalizes value fields, and emits a decision_decided_signal for decision_take.

symbols.does: Reads the direction marks written at an address.
symbols.description: A thin, degrade-clean wrapper around suite.marks_for that returns an empty list on error so re-renders never crash.
